explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while fats are the biggest drag. It lands in C tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in C tier with an overall score of 37 from a base nutrition score of 37. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: easy protein delivery for normal diets
topPros.explanation: Dairy gets real credit for convenience.

topPros.title: clear calcium identity
topPros.explanation: That is a core category strength.

topPros.title: slots into meals very easily
topPros.explanation: Practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

topCons.title: not everyone tolerates dairy well
topCons.explanation: That is a real use-case limitation.

topCons.title: fat quality varies wildly across dairy
topCons.explanation: Some dairy is clean, some really is not.

topCons.title: processing changes the category a lot
topCons.explanation: The label alone does not tell the whole story.

closing.summary: Feta stands out for easy protein delivery for normal diets and clear calcium identity. but it also gets held back by not everyone tolerates dairy well and fat quality varies wildly across dairy. Best only when the taste or format matters more than getting the strongest dairy profile.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when the taste or format matters more than getting the strongest dairy profile.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
